titleEn: Arkansas team develops robot to handle poultry carcasses
titleZh: 阿肯色团队开发家禽胴体处理机器人
summaryZh: 阿肯色农业实验站的研究人员开发了名为ChicGrasp的机器人系统，利用模仿学习算法和摄像头感知，通过双颚夹具抓取鸡胴体并挂上挂钩输送线。该系统旨在应对家禽加工厂劳动力短缺，目前成功率约81%，但单次循环需38秒（人工约3秒）。原型成本约5.9万美元，研究获美国农业部与 NSF 100万美元资助，代码和设计已开源。
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了模仿学习与具身AI在家禽加工自动化中的前沿应用，直接回应行业劳动力短缺痛点，开源设计为智慧畜牧从业者提供了可复现的技术参考。
contentFull: Processing Equipment & innovation Digital innovation Arkansas team develops robot to handle poultry carcasses Imitation-learning system mimics human movements in plants 12 March 2026 1 minute read By: Global Ag Media North America Researchers at the Arkansas Agricultural Experiment Station have developed a robotic system that can learn by imitating human movements to handle chickens on poultry processing lines, according to a news release from Arkansas State University. The system, called ChicGrasp, uses an advanced imitation learning algorithm and camera perceptions to guide a dual-jaw robotic gripper with pinchers that can grasp a chicken carcass by the legs, lift it and hang it on a shackle conveyor for further processing.The technology was initially developed in response to labour shortages in poultry processing plants during the COVID-19 pandemic."Embodied AI is used to create intelligent, agent-like robotics to interact with a real-world environment," said Dongyi Wang, leader of the project and an assistant professor in the Departments of Biological and Agricultural Engineering and Food Science."It's a physical art that has just developed in the past couple of years, which you see in things like full self-driving cars," he said. "We are trying to do similar things using that imitation learning idea, but in chicken processing."The project has been supported by a $1 million grant from a joint program between the U.S. Department of Agriculture’s National Institute of Food and Agriculture and the National Science Foundation. Results of the study were published in Advanced Robotics Research, and the team released all design files, code and datasets as open source.The researchers designed the system to address challenges faced by traditional robotics on poultry processing lines, where birds can be cold, slippery and irregular in size and posture."In imitation learning, the role of the human is to give a trajectory, give a ground truth to the robot, so we don't need to start from scratch to learn," said Amirreza Davar, a graduate student who designed the gripper and modified the learning system. "It's more efficient and more accurate. From the get-go, the robot knows what we need to do."So far, ChicGrasp has demonstrated a success rate of nearly 81%. However, speed remains a challenge. While a human worker can hang a chicken carcass on a conveyor in about three seconds, the full cycle for ChicGrasp currently takes about 38 seconds.The prototype system cost about $59,000 to build using off-the-shelf robotic arm hardware and 3D-printed components. Researchers said future work will focus on improving speed and refining the algorithm to move the technology closer to industrial use. translationZh:
阿肯色农业实验站的研究人员开发了一种机器人系统，可通过模仿人类动作来学习处理家禽加工线上的鸡，据阿肯色州立大学发布的新闻稿称。

该系统名为ChicGrasp，采用先进的模仿学习算法和摄像头感知，引导一个带有钳子的双颚机器人夹具，能够抓住鸡胴体的腿部，将其提起并挂到挂钩输送线上进行后续加工。

该技术最初是为应对新冠疫情期间家禽加工厂的劳动力短缺而开发的。

**“具身AI用于创建智能的、类似代理的机器人，以与现实世界环境交互，”** 项目负责人、生物与农业工程及食品科学系助理教授王东义说。

**“这是一种在过去几年才发展起来的物理艺术，你可以在全自动驾驶汽车等事物中看到它，”** 他说。**“我们正尝试利用这种模仿学习理念做类似的事情，但应用在鸡肉加工中。”**

该项目得到了美国农业部国家食品与农业研究所与美国国家科学基金会联合项目提供的100万美元资助。研究结果发表在《先进机器人研究》期刊上，研究团队已将全部设计文件、代码和数据集作为开源资源发布。

研究人员设计该系统是为了应对家禽加工线上传统机器人面临的挑战，这些加工线上的鸡只可能冰冷、湿滑，且大小和姿态不规则。

**“在模仿学习中，人类的角色是为机器人提供轨迹，提供真实基准，这样我们就不必从头开始学习，”** 设计夹具并修改学习系统的研究生Amirreza Davar说。**“它更高效、更准确。从一开始，机器人就知道我们需要做什么。”**

到目前为止，ChicGrasp的成功率已接近81%。然而，速度仍然是一个挑战。人类工人将鸡胴体挂上输送线大约需要3秒，而ChicGrasp的完整循环目前需要约38秒。

该原型系统使用现成的机械臂硬件和3D打印组件建造，成本约为5.9万美元。研究人员表示，未来的工作将集中在提高速度和优化算法上，以使该技术更接近工业应用。
contentHtml:
Arkansas team develops robot to handle poultry carcasses 
Imitation-learning system mimics human movements in plants
